name: A167 DURHAM WESTERN BYPASS 1/18
units: metres below ground level; typed BGS/Wimpey Geotech cable-percussion site-investigation log, depths as recorded. OD levels cross-checked against 81.10 mAOD datum.

intervals.verbatim: TOPSOIL
Stiff orange brown slightly sandy silty CLAY with some fine to coarse angular to subrounded gravel
Stiff mottled orange brown and grey slightly sandy silty CLAY with some fine to coarse angular to subrounded gravel
Loose pale brown very silty fine SAND with occasional black carbonaceous pocket and occasional gravel of coal
annotations: 'gravel of coal' = reworked coal detritus in drift, not an in-situ seam

intervals.verbatim: Loose and loose to medium dense brown silty fine to coarse SAND with occasional gravel of coal
Loose brown silty fine SAND with occasional brown very silty clay pockets and occasional gravel of coal
Pale brown clayey sandy SILT with occasional silty clay lamination: faintly laminated in places
annotations: soft glaciolacustrine silt (drift), not siltstone bedrock

intervals.verbatim: Brown silty fine SAND with some pockets of brown laminated sandy silt and occasional gravel of coal
Laminated brown silty CLAY with some silt and fine sand partings
Medium dense brown slightly clayey silty fine and medium SAND with occasional pale brown clayey silt pockets and occasional gravel of coal
Medium dense brown and pale brown silty fine and medium SAND with occasional laminated reddish brown silty clay and occasional gravel of coal
annotations: with water: first ground-water struck at 13.50m (GWe), rose 0.70m in 30 min, cut off by casing at 15.50m

intervals.verbatim: Laminated brown silty CLAY with some silt and fine sand pockets
Brown fine and medium SAND with some laminated brown silty clay pockets
Firm laminated brown silty CLAY with some silt and fine sand pockets: pocket of brown silty clay with some angular to subrounded gravel at 15.25m to 16.35m
Stiff greyish brown sandy silty CLAY with some fine to coarse angular to subrounded gravel and occasional cobble
Very dense brown silty fine to coarse SAND with some fine and medium angular to subrounded gravel and occasional gravel of coal
Dense orange brown silty fine and medium SAND with occasional fine angular gravel and occasional gravel of coal: driller noted occasional bands of brown sandy silty clay
Greyish brown sandy silty CLAY with some fine to coarse angular to subrounded gravel and occasional gravel of coal

Medium dense silty fine and medium SAND with occasional fine to coarse angular gravel occasional gravel of coal occasional cobble occasional brown silty clay pocket and occasional brown clayey sandy silt pocket
Laminated brown silty CLAY with some fine and medium angular to subrounded gravel and occasional fine and medium sand parting
Very dense brown silty fine and medium SAND with some gravel of coal
annotations: with water: second ground-water seepage struck at 22.80m (GWe), rose 0.90m in 30 min, cut off by casing at 28.00m

intervals.verbatim: Brown sandy silty CLAY with some fine to coarse angular to subrounded gravel and occasional gravel of coal
Dense orange brown silty fine and medium SAND with some pockets of laminated silty clay
Brown fine to coarse SAND and fine to coarse angular and subangular GRAVEL with occasional cobble

Very stiff dark brown sandy silty CLAY with some fine to coarse angular gravel and some pockets of laminated brown silty clay with some silt and fine sand partings below 26.45m: cobble at 27.40m to 27.55m
Stiff laminated brown silty CLAY with some silt and fine sand partings
Stiff brown sandy silty CLAY with some fine to coarse angular to subrounded gravel and occasional gravel of coal
annotations: with water: third ground-water seepage struck at 30.90m (GWe), rose 2.50m in 30 min

intervals.verbatim: Brown fine to coarse SAND and fine to coarse angular to subrounded GRAVEL
Stiff brown sandy silty CLAY with some fine to coarse angular to subrounded gravel

notes: Modern typed Wimpey Geotech metric log (Lab Ref S/26671; Fig 19), cable percussion with Pilcon Wayfarer, commenced 15/6/92; 4 strata sheets (Sheet 1-4 of 4). The entire 31.80m is Quaternary drift (a thick glacial/glaciofluvial sequence of clays, sands, silts and gravels typical of the Wear lowlands); bedrock (Coal Measures) was NOT reached, so there are no coal seams and seam_marks is empty. The recurrent 'gravel of coal' / 'carbonaceous pocket' entries are reworked coal detritus within the drift, not in-situ seams. No pencil seam marginalia (this is a typed record, not a Victorian ledger). OD levels printed at every boundary cross-check EXACTLY against the 81.10 mOD datum minus depth (verified incl. 66.80 at 14.30m, 66.60 at 14.50m); no clerk arithmetic slips found. Three ground-water seepages recorded end-of-log and annotated per interval: 13.50m (rose 0.70m/30min, cased off 15.50m), 22.80m (rose 0.90m/30min, cased off 28.00m), 30.90m (rose 2.50m/30min); a lockable protective steel cover was concreted over the piezometer installation. Chiselling logged at 23.95-24.30m (1.00h), 25.10-25.40m (1.00h), 26.10-26.40m (0.75h) and 30.40-30.80m (1.50h) through hard gravel/cobble bands. Pages 5-8 are Roughton & Partners site/location plans (Durham Western Bypass) with no strata; only pages 1-4 carry log data. Boring diameter 200mm to 15.00m then 150mm to 31.80m; casing 200mm to 15.00m then 150mm to 30.00m.
